items.title: Concerns about cricket pitches on ovals after Melbourne football tragedy

children.content: Nathan Fitzgerald's club has asked authorities to look at the way hard cricket pitches are covered on multi-use ovals as the 27-year-old continues to receive end-of-life care.

gallery.altText: A football club's image of a 27-year-old on a blue background.

thumbnail.altText: A football club's image of a 27-year-old on a blue background.

items.title: AFP confirms threat made against Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi

children.content: Australian Federal Police says a young person was identified and issued a formal warning over an online threat made towards Mr Modi, ahead of his official visit later this week.

image.altText: Man wearing suit shakes hands with a second man, standing in front of a wall with two different flags on it.

items.title: 'Pretty rare to see clearance rates this low': Property slump tipped to continue

children.content: Less than half of all properties that went to auction in Australia were actually sold over the last week.

image.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

items.title: Earthquake rocks community along Victoria's Great Ocean Road

children.content: The magnitude-3.8 earthquake struck at a depth of 10 kilometres along Victoria's popular Great Ocean Road.

image.altText: A map showing an earthquake location

items.title: Man's body pulled from Murray River at Barooga

children.content: NSW Police are investigating the death of a man who was found in the Murray River.

image.altText: Images of blue and white checkered police tape stretched across a suburban street.

items.title: Retail strip caught up in arson attack on inner Melbourne tobacco shop

children.content: More than 80 firefighters were called to Richmond where multiple businesses were alight after a shop was rammed this morning.

gallery.altText: A police officer walks down the middle of a city street where a fire truck is parked and flames can be seen coming from a shop.
Firefighters wearing helmets stand on a smoke filled street between a police car and fire engine with water coming from hoses.
A person walks through thick smoke over a city road talking into a walkie talkie near a fire truck and firefighters at night.

items.title: Man critical after car fire at Scoresby in Melbourne's east

children.content: Police are investigating a car fire that left a man with life-threatening burns in Melbourne's east.

image.altText: A white car that says "paramedic" is parked across a narrow road where police cars are parked at night.

items.title: Broken back prompts apprentice electrician to chase footy career

children.content: Rather than ending his promising sporting prospects, a fractured back has prompted Mount Gambier tall Jobe Janeway to chase his dream of playing in the AFL.

image.altText: Jobe Janeway runs out with the South Australian team for a game against the Allies in the national championships

items.title: Footballer in end-of-life care after head clash in suburban game

children.content: Epping Football Netball Club's Nathan Fitzgerald, 27, is receiving end-of-life care after colliding with a teammate in a reserves game.

gallery.altText: A football club's image of a 27-year-old on a blue background.

thumbnail.altText: A football club's image of a 27-year-old on a blue background.

items.title: Fran Lebowitz — witty, withering and unfiltered

children.content: No-one emerges unscathed from Fran Lebowitz's quips, bon mots and scathing take-downs of New York's peculiarities and poor etiquette.

image.altText: Close up shot of Fran Lebowitz staring down the lens, on stage against a blue curtain wearing a navy jacket and white shirt

items.title: Search continues for woman missing in Melbourne's south

children.content: Police found a car belonging to Yun, also known as Michelle, in a car park on Friday but no other signs of her whereabouts.

image.altText: A CCTV image of a dark haired woman wearing a black top and black pants walks between a petrol bowser and a white car.

items.title: Winmar statue removed during NAIDOC Week after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

gallery.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

thumbnail.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

items.title: Magpie Maynard makes contact with umpire after being hit 'cheaply'

children.content: Collingwood defender Brayden Maynard could be in trouble after making contact with an umpire during the Magpies' six-point win over the Gold Coast Suns.

image.altText: Brayden Maynard of the Magpies is knocked down, holding his ribs

items.title: Investors 'all but gone' from Melbourne property market after budget

children.content: With clearance rates in Melbourne sitting just above 50 per cent, experts say sluggish sales show investors have lost trust in bricks and mortar.

image.altText: A man jogs past a brown brick house that has a large sign attached to the fence indicating it is for sale.

items.title: When Jackie Chan came to Falls Creek and got hypothermia in a frozen lake

children.content: Thirty years ago a Hollywood crew turned Falls Creek into Ukraine, and Jackie Chan nearly froze getting the shot.

gallery.altText: Jackie chan in snow outfit and seal beanie smile and stands behind a video camera.

thumbnail.altText: Jackie chan in snow outfit and seal beanie smile and stands behind a video camera.

items.title: 15yo honoured at memorial as third teenager charged with his murder

children.content: Darweish Mohamed, who was allegedly stabbed to death outside a Melbourne hospital on Wednesday, has been laid to rest as family and friends remembered him at a memorial today.

image.altText: Young man looks at camera in diagonal shot

items.title: Arson chemist investigates fatal house fire in Melbourne's south-east

children.content: A man believed to be in his 60s died after being pulled from a room engulfed in flames in a Springvale South home overnight.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Thunderbirds win record-breaking Super Netball title

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds have unlocked a Super Netball dynasty with a unique coaching approach under Tania Obst. The three-time Super Netball champions defeated the Melbourne Vixens 61- 40 in the grand final at John Cain Arena.

image.altText: A women's sports team poses together on a stage with a trophy and pink confetti

items.title: Four rescued from roof of stranded 4WD in flooded river

children.content: Four people are rescued from floodwaters after their 4WD is swept into a river crossing.

image.altText: The police air wing lifts stranded occupants from a flooded river

items.title: 'A lot to look forward to': Fans pay tribute to Socceroos despite World Cup elimination

children.content: Football fans across the country ride the gamut of emotions as the Socceroos suffer heartbreaking defeat in the FIFA World Cup, having come from behind to force extra time and penalties against Egypt in their knockout clash in Texas.

image.altText: Lucas Herrington looks disappointed

articles.title: Ange Postecoglou appointed head coach at Saudi club Al-Nassr

children.content: The 60-year-old takes over the Riyadh-based side led by Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo.

image.altText: A man looks out onto a football field.

items.title: Meeting to decide Moira Deeming's future cancelled amid court battle

children.content: Victorian MP Moira Deeming has won a two-week reprieve as a Liberal candidate for the November state election, after the party agreed in the Supreme Court to cancel imminent plans to revoke her preselection.

image.altText: Liberal party MP Moira Deeming exits from a joint party meeting, wearing a blue dress.

items.title: Woman who attacked Senator Lidia Thorpe outside the MCG avoids jail

children.content: Senator Thorpe was with a group of friends near the stadium after the Dreamtime at the G game between Essendon and Richmond when she was attacked by Ebony Jan Bell.

image.altText: Ebony Bell leaves Magistrates Court in Melbourne

items.title: Charges dropped against two teens accused of abducting girl near school

children.content: Victoria Police have dropped charges against two males who were arrested and charged with the abduction of a teenage girl outside a Melbourne school.

image.altText: A blue sleeve with a Victoria Police badge sewn into it, including the motto "Uphold the Right".

items.title: Patients at risk from 'toxic' mental health service, clinicians warn

children.content: Psychiatrist Dr Sarah Dahlenburg was so worried about her region's mental health service she warned her bosses. Two months later, a patient was dead.

image.altText: Two nurses walk down a hospital corridor seen from behind.

items.title: 175 years ago, Victoria struck gold. 230,000 scars remain

children.content: It has been 175 years since gold was first discovered in Victoria, and now a state government mapping project has discovered that there are far more relics of the gold rush left in the landscape than official records show.

gallery.altText: A person in high vis looks at a tablet next to a hole covered by a grate
A black and white photograph of children sitting on a mullock heap on the outskirts of town
Louise kneels on the ground in the bush, wearing high vis

Louise kneels on the ground in the bush, wearing high vis

items.title: Childcare safety loophole for educators breaching regulations

children.content: A loophole in the government’s new childcare safety legislation allows educators who have breached safety standards to continue operating.

image.altText: A rubber giraffe-duck, teddy bear and various coloured hoops are seen among other toys.

items.title: Firefighters' union loses Supreme Court bid to keep corruption probe secret

children.content: An investigation into allegations of corruption during the tenure of former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews was finished in 2022, but its release has been delayed by court action.

image.altText: Peter Marshall wears a navy and yellow

items.title: Victorian government to review puppy farm laws amid 'loophole' concerns

children.content: The Victorian government will review animal welfare laws to tighten loopholes being "exploited" by puppy farmers.

image.altText: Georgie posing and smiling with a dog.

items.title: Worst start to ski season since 1957, as blizzard finally dumps snow

children.content: Following the worst start to a ski season in decades, a major snowstorm hit the NSW and Victorian Alps last night.

image.altText: Perisher field with snow, dark shadows of trees, on a dark winter's afternoon.

items.title: Tens of thousands of Victorian drivers wrongly charged for Linkt tolls

children.content: Toll company Linkt says up to 90,000 motorists may have been over or undercharged after driving on CityLink or the West Gate Tunnel.

gallery.altText: Traffic driving down a freeway, photographed at a slow speed so the cars look blurry.

thumbnail.altText: Traffic driving down a freeway, photographed at a slow speed so the cars look blurry.

items.title: Exhibition shines light on Australian women without housing

children.content: Women and girls make up 60 per cent of the estimated 289,000 people who seek help across Australia, according to the latest statistics from the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare.

image.altText: A woman gestures while seated opposite another person in a social work setting.

items.title: Teenager charged with murder after death in Melbourne's north

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been charged with murder after a boy was found with stab wounds outside a Craigieburn hospital.

image.altText: Craigieburn Community Hospital

items.title: 'Robbing people using visas': Applicants shocked by sudden price hike

children.content: International students and families say they feel shocked and exploited after visa fees across dozens of categories rose on July 1 without warning, with some surging more than 200 per cent.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses and a white chef's uniform is stir-frying at the stove.

items.title: XPeng electric vehicle owners still chasing $5k cashback months on

children.content: When Mustansir Milky took the leap and bought a new electric vehicle, he didn't expect to have to fight to get the $5,000 cashback payment he was promised.

image.altText: Mustansir Milky stands beside his electric car outside his house

items.title: Traditional owner takes government to court over flood plain project

children.content: A protest camp on the banks of the Murray River enters its sixth week as traditional owners and locals fight a plan to return water to nine flood plains they say will kill trees and wildlife.
